68b30046abe4e93dffa35924.question: This event’s date was delayed every two years by the insertion of an intercalary month that pushed it to five weeks after Terminalia, though the high priest could also choose not to. The craftsman of eleven shields is the namesake of a festival that either coincided with this event or came one day before in which an old man was wrapped in hides and beaten with sticks. The common people would gather at a grove a mile down the Via Flaminia on this date to celebrate Anna Perenna, whose name alludes to the time before the reign of Numa Pompilius when it had the first full moon of the year. The death of Attis was celebrated on this <u>day</u> while his rebirth was on the vernal equinox. For 10 points, name this date sixteen days before the kalends of Aprilis.

The common people would gather at a grove a mile down the Via Flaminia on this date to celebrate Anna Perenna, whose name alludes to the time before the reign of Numa Pompilius when it had the first full moon of the year. The death of Attis was celebrated on this day while his rebirth was on the vernal equinox. For 10 points, name this date sixteen days before the kalends of Aprilis.
68b30046abe4e93dffa35924.answer: ides of March [Idus Martiae, 15th of March, 15th of Martius; prompt on ides]

68b30046abe4e93dffa35927.question: This figure smiles cheekily as they poke their head in frame on Folio 135 of the Nuremberg Chronicle, which gave them a halo to match a person in a bishop’s hat.This figure was left entirely unpainted near the stone-holding subject in an incomplete work that was cut up before it was identified by Cardinal Fesch as a Leonardo. This figure sits next to Albert of Brandenburg in four portraits as a saint by Cranach the Elder. This figure is a spindly-legged silhouette in the colonnade on the right of a painting by Antonello da Messina that is framed by a stone arch. The story of this figure may have been borrowed wholesale from Androcles and appeared for the first time in the Golden <u>Legend</u>. This figure lies next to a sleeping dog in one of the Meisterstiche showing a saint writing diligently. For 10 points, name this creature that was tamed by having a thorn plucked from its paw.

68b30046abe4e93dffa35928.question: Tina Modotti thought of these objects "and embryos" after seeing Edward Weston's shell photos. A Miguel Covarrubias caricature of an artist nicknamed for these objects shows them in profile with closed eyes and was published the year after the anonymous sale of $25,000 worth of paintings of them. Two of these objects emerge from a shell in Charles Demuth's cryptic portrait of "female impersonator" Bert Savoy, which borrows them from Marsden Hartley as a symbol of homosexuality. One artist's take on these flowers often uses tall canvases to highlight their long, green <u>stems</u>, but others are more characteristically zoomed-in. For 10 points, name these white flowers which are synonymous with the early work of Georgia O'Keeffe.
